Summary of the navigation: I started in the closet, correctly walked forward and turned right through the arched door into the master bedroom. From there, the instruction's "turn right and see bathroom" proved difficult — this master suite had an unusually complex layout with multiple walk-in closets, arched doorways, and a chain of domed tiled foyers. I explored the right-turn passage (closet), the forward hallway (which led to grand rotunda/entry foyers), and several arched openings, but never found a clearly-identifiable bathroom with an exposed tub and sink (the most likely bathroom appeared to be behind a closed, non-traversable wooden door adjacent to the bedroom).

Recognizing I had overshot into the formal areas, I rewound back to the bedroom and, with budget going critical, made my stand in the bedroom beside the arched doorways leading to the lighter tiled/vanity room — my best remaining bathroom candidate — and issued STOP there rather than ending with no stop (which scores zero).
